refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency is within 150 ms p95 to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR specifies a high-level latency requirement for teleoperation. Refining it to explicitly state the latency threshold and its impact on safety clarifies the requirement and enables targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end communication latency under agreed network conditions and verify that the 95th percentile latency does not exceed 150 ms during teleoperation sessions.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable and timely data transmission over the network link.
FR3: Provide real-time telemetry and tasking interfaces with low latency.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize communication protocol stack for low latency.
DP2: Use dedicated network link with QoS guarantees.
DP3: Implement edge processing to reduce data transmission volume.
DP4: Prioritize teleoperation data traffic in system scheduling.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP2: Use dedicated network link with QoS guarantees.

potential_sources: Dependency on network link performance and availability.
Interaction with safety services for alerting on latency breaches.
coupling_check.notes: The DP focuses on network link QoS, which is a clear and direct solution to the latency FR. Coupling with other DPs is minimal and manageable.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions can be controlled or guaranteed as per agreed SLA.
Risk that network QoS may degrade under unforeseen conditions affecting latency.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating edge processing (DP3) if network QoS alone is insufficient.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing teleoperation communication, including network link management and latency control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for data transmission between teleoperation module and vehicle/system network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides real-time telemetry data to other modules and stakeholders.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands and tasking instructions.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused on teleoperation specifics; higher diffusion risk.
Primarily physical vehicle control; less suitable for network latency management.
Highest containment score; focused responsibility reduces diffusion risk.
Safety monitoring role; not responsible for latency control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the latency control DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes responsibility and minimizes diffusion, enabling clear ownership and interface management.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on network link performance outside module control.
Risk of interface complexity if Teleoperation module must coordinate extensively with Vehicle and Safety Services.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and monitoring mechanisms with Network Link and Safety Services to mitigate risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (dedicated network link with QoS) directly addresses the refined FR with clear assumptions and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk due to focused Teleoperation module ownership.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best IM boundary for this DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be measured end-to-end via Teleoperation module interfaces and network monitoring tools.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Enhance interface definitions between Teleoperation and Network Link modules.
Consider fallback or mitigation strategies if network QoS degrades.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignment meets the requirement with clear ownership, manageable risks, and feasible verification. No further decomposition needed at this level.

revise.why: Current FR, DP, and IM are well aligned and satisfy the acceptance criteria.

assumptions: Network conditions can be guaranteed as per SLA.
Teleoperation module can manage network link QoS effectively.
evidence_needed: Latency performance data under operational network conditions.
